Organization Overview 
Grant Thornton – A Global Organisation 
Grant Thornton International Ltd (GTIL) is one of the world’s leading networks of independent 
assurance, tax, and advisory firms. Member firms share a commitment to delivering high-quality, 
value-driven solutions across diverse industries. GTIL supports dynamic organisations from 
fast-growing businesses to global enterprises by combining international reach, deep sector 
expertise, and a collaborative approach to help clients navigate complexity, seize opportunities, 
and achieve sustainable growth. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Seamlessly Global, Distinctly Bharat 
Grant Thornton Bharat LLP (GTBLLP), a founding member of the global Grant Thornton 
network, is one of India’s leading consulting firms. Since 2000, we have been delivering end-to-
end solutions in assurance, tax, technology, managed services, deals, ESG, and risk consulting. 
We work with mid-market companies, large corporates, government bodies, and digital-first 
businesses, combining global expertise with deep local insight. Guided by our #GoBeyond 
philosophy, we are committed to empowering our people, delivering exceptional value to clients, 
and contributing to the growth of a Vibrant Bharat. 
 
 
 
Our Transformation Consulting (TFC)  
Grant Thornton Bharat’s Transformation Consulting helps organisations future-proof operations 
and drive sustainable growth through end-to-end transformation strategies. We work with 
leadership to reimagine business models, optimise processes, and embed digital excellence. 
Our expertise spans process re-engineering, operating model redesign, program management, 
performance improvement, digital enablement, and change management delivering sector-
specific solutions that create agility, resilience, and lasting impact.

pages.7:
Proposal for Providing IT Related Manpower to GEL 
7 
 
Unlocking India’s Potential: Partnering to Deliver on Government 
Priorities 
 
Grant Thornton is widely recognised as a trusted partner, distinguished by our unwavering commitment 
to reliability, quality, and integrity. Through active engagement with national policy forums, we offer 
forward-looking insights into the evolving business and regulatory landscape. This strategic foresight 
enables us to equip our clients with the tools and approaches needed to anticipate change, respond 
proactively, and achieve sustainable growth in a dynamic environment. 
 
 
We are closely aligned with the Government of India’s vision of building a Vibrant Bharat. Leveraging 
our deep multisectoral experience, robust technical capabilities, and proven project management 
expertise, we bring a holistic perspective to every engagement. Our team, comprising seasoned internal 
professionals and domain experts has extensive experience delivering large-scale assignments and 
flagship programs across the country. 
 
With a nuanced understanding of the complexities unique to the public sector, we are committed to 
ensuring that intended outcomes are fully realised. Our public sector practice collaborates with 
government bodies and public institutions to enhance operational efficiencies, drive ICT-led 
transformation, foster economic growth and investment, ensure compliance with international 
standards, and improve citizen service delivery. 
Our clientele includes a broad spectrum of international development agencies such as The World Bank 
Group, Asian Development Bank, Japan International Cooperation Agency, UN agencies, Agence 
Française de Développement (AFD Group), GiZ, and bilateral organisations like the Foreign, 
Commonwealth and Development Office and the Global Green Growth Institute. Our multidisciplinary 
experts are dedicated to advancing a safe, sustainable, and resilient future, reducing environmental 
impact, supporting the transition to a carbon-neutral economy, and strengthening community resilience 
to climate change and related risks. 
Grant Thornton’s steadfast commitment to public value, sustainable development, and innovative 
solutions positions us as a preferred partner for governments and institutions alike. Together, we are 
shaping a resilient and forward-looking Bharat, one built on collaboration, expertise, and a shared vision 
for a better tomorrow
pages.8:
Proposal for Providing IT Related Manpower to GEL 
8 
 
 
 
Digigov Group, under Transformation Consulting at Grant Thornton Bharat LLP, is aiming to 
create a resilient and innovative governance ecosystem. 
  
The Digigov Group revolves around three core offerings: harnessing cutting-edge 
technologies for streamlined governance, driving economic transformation and enhancing 
disaster resilience. 
The vision is to build a sustainable and responsive governance framework that meets the 
challenges of today and prepares for the opportunities of tomorrow. 
 
 
DigiGov 
 
 
• 
Government automation/E-governance: Uses information and communication 
technology (ICT) to streamline administrative functions to improve service delivery to 
citizens, businesses, and other stakeholders. 
 
• 
India Enterprise Architecture framework: Assists government entities in adopting 
and implementing the India Enterprise Architecture (IndEA) framework to achieve the 
vision of a digitally integrated government enterprise. 
 
• 
Government cyber tech: Focuses on enhancing cybersecurity capabilities and 
safeguarding critical infrastructure, spanning the full spectrum of cyber technology 
services, from strategy development to operational implementation. 
 
• 
New age tech – government: Designs solutions to empower government 
organisations with cutting-edge technologies and innovative approaches to address 
complex challenges and deliver citizen-centric services.  
 
• 
Tech solutions (Alliances/custom build): Offers a wide array of tech solutions by 
partnering with top technology firms and developing bespoke solutions designed to 
address each client's specific requirements, utilising cutting-edge technologies for 
digital advancement and improved operational effectiveness. 
 
• 
Government enterprise apps (Energy and MM): Designs applications to address the 
specific requirements and obstacles encountered by government agencies managing 
energy, materials, and resources, aiming to improve operations, efficiency, and 
resource utilisation.

4. Experience in Software Development 
Project 1: Project Management Unit – Digital Citizen Services and Beneficiary Management 
System (BMS) 
Item (1) 
Particulars of the Project (2) 
Name of the Project 
Project Management Unit – Digital Citizen Services and 
Beneficiary Management System (BMS) 
Client Name for whom the services 
were provided 
Planning Investment Promotion and Sustainable 
Development Department, Govt of Meghalaya 
Cost of the project 
9.94 Cr 
Project Start Date / LOA 
September 2023 
Project End Date 
August 2026 
Nature of the Project (Give a brief 
description for better understanding 
relevant to experience requirement for 
this project 
The PMU will undertake an in-depth study of the processes 
involved in seeking services and benefits from the 
government under all departmental services and prepare 
Business Architecture. The architecture would also cover 
citizen centric demand/ valued services running either 
manually or through a semi-online process as well. The 
consultant would make Application Architecture which would 
include technology platform that can automate these 
processes. The PMU would prepare Data Architecture 
ensuring the incorporation of Beneficiary ID for delivery of all 
services. 
Scope of the work for which the Bidder 
was responsible 
• 
Process analysis and re-engineering required to 
transform existing processes (BPR/GPR) for enhanced 
delivery of citizen centric services and schemes. 
• 
Process analysis required for transforming and 
mainstreaming, 
citizen 
services/processes 
(both 
online/semi-online and manual) 
• 
Alignment of services to Meghalaya Enterprise 
Architecture 
• 
Preparation of FRS/SRS, system requirements for a 
platform design including a mobile based app that will 
be developed. 
• 
Identify and analyse the comprehensive beneficiary 
data in the state and identify challenges associated with 
them. 
• 
Derive an architecture and operational mechanism for 
the BMS Data Architecture 
• 
Regulatory Changes, Standard Operating Procedures & 
Policies 
Implementation 
Methodology, 
Budgetary 
Requirements, Project Timelines & Arrangements 
• 
Analytics Support for Beneficiary Management System 
• 
Ideate analytics reporting requirement 
• 
Support in designing dashboard by providing functional 
requirements 
• 
Prepare reports for Senior Stakeholder review meetings 
• 
Prepare predictive analytics reports from existing data 
• 
Support department in data mining to identify key 
scheme delivery gaps 
• 
Development of Request for Proposal Document 
• 
The consultants shall develop a full request for proposal 
document, adhering to the procurement guidelines

Project 2: Agency for Designing, Development and Operations & Management 
of MIDH-SURAKSHA 
Item (1) 
Particulars of the Project (2) 
Name of the Project 
Agency for Designing, Development and Operations & 
Management of MIDH-SURAKSHA 
Client Name for whom the services 
were provided 
National Horticulture Board, Ministry of Agriculture and 
Farmers Welfare, Govt. of India 
Cost of the project 
3.65 Cr. 
Project Start Date / LOA 
April 2024 
Project End Date 
April 2027 
Nature of the Project (Give a brief 
description for better understanding 
relevant to experience requirement for 
this project 
The project aims to integrate the existing IT applications into 
a 
single 
unified 
and 
integrated 
application 
MIDH-
SURAKSHA that will act as the primary source of monitoring 
for MIDH Scheme as a whole. A brief about these existing 
applications are as follows in upcoming sections. 
Scope of the work for which the Bidder 
was responsible 
• Evaluate the existing HAPIS & HORTNET platforms and 
identify areas for improvement in terms of performance, 
data visualization, and dashboard management. 
• Redesign the HAPIS & HORTNET platforms using 
modern frontend frameworks to enhance user experience 
and interactivity which will be a part of MIDH-SURAKSHA 
• Design and develop a user-friendly and responsive web 
portal using modern web development technologies. 
• Implement dynamic data visualization tools, such as 
charts and graphs, to provide comprehensive insights into 
horticulture data. 
• Integrate APIs such as Aadhaar Validation API, PM-
KISAN API, SMS and email APIs, geo-tagging and geo-
fencing, LGD databases to enhance the functionality and 
data exchange of the portal. 
• Develop and integrate a system for managing state-
specific data, ensuring seamless integration with the core 
system for efficient data handling. 
• Use Krishi Mapper for GIS-based land capturing and 
database management to improve data accuracy and 
accessibility. 
• Develop a training plan with user manuals and workshops 
to educate state and district bodies, adapting to their 
needs, and gather feedback for ongoing improvement. 
• Develop a comprehensive training plan to impart 
necessary skills and knowledge to state and district 
bodies. 
• Conduct PeriodicTraining sessions and workshops to 
familiarize 
users 
with 
the 
portal's 
features 
and 
functionalities in States/Districts as per the requirement/ 
need basis.

Project 3: Project Management Unit & Knowledge Partner for MyMeG Program 
Item (1) 
Particulars of the Project (2) 
Name of the Project 
Project Management Unit & Knowledge Partner for 
MyMeG Program 
Client Name for whom the services 
were provided 
Planning Department, Government of Meghalaya 
Cost of the project 
10.06 Cr. 
Project Start Date / LOA 
December 2022 
Project End Date 
December 2025 
Nature of the Project (Give a brief 
description for better understanding 
relevant to experience requirement for 
this project 
Planning Department, Government of Meghalaya had 
initiated MyMeG project, a citizen engagement program to 
bring transparency and encourage participatory governance 
in policies and issues concerning the State. 
Through the project the Government envisions to achieve 
following objectives: 
• 
Enhance transparency of the Government by 
including citizens in the monitoring of Government 
schemes and programs  
• 
Engage citizens through multiple channels in policy 
making, progress monitoring, information and 
advisory service disseminations through feedback 
and ideation challenges 
• 
Empower citizens by enabling them to have their 
voices heard, information captured and need 
assessed. 
• 
Encourage citizen participation by way of 
connecting with the communities, societies and 
various local groups of Meghalaya to discover key 
interventions that are required to foster inclusive 
and holistic development.  
Main Features of the project:- 
 
• 
Scheme beneficiary database management – 
Coordination and liaisoning with over 15 
departments in the capacity of knowledge 
partner for the government of Meghalaya. Data 
Collection, analysis, reports preparation. Creation of 
Booklets/leaflets, hoardings and banners for 
Government to citizen communication. 
• 
Designed, implemented and maintaining the 
MyMeG Dashboard that provides details on the 
scheme delivery in the state. The dashboard is also 
integrated with beneficiary testimonials. 
• 
Support to the Government in implementation 
of various initiatives like FOCUS, FOCUS+, 
YESS Meghalaya and Citizen Engagement & 
Communication Program (CECP) and associated 
capacity building. 
• 
Vendor Management and Handling of major 
launch events and CM interactive events 
 
 
Scope of the work for which the Bidder 
was responsible 
• 
Data analysis of schemes and its benefits to the 
citizens. Gather data from departments related to 
schemes, understand impact of scheme delivery

Proposal for Providing IT Related Manpower to GEL

Project 4: IT PROFESSIONALS FOR APPLICATION DEVELOPMENT AND 
MAINTENANCE FOR PFMS (TOTAL 162 IT PROFESSIONALS) 
 
Item (1) 
Particulars of the Project (2) 
Name of the Project 
IT PROFESSIONALS FOR APPLICATION 
DEVELOPMENT AND MAINTENANCE FOR PFMS 
(TOTAL 162 IT PROFESSIONALS) 
Client Name for whom the services 
were provided 
Public Financial Management System (PFMS), Controller 
General of Accounts 
Cost of the project 
104 Crore (including GST) 
Project Start Date / LOA 
July 2024 
Project End Date 
July 2027 
Nature of the Project (Give a brief 
description for better understanding 
relevant to experience requirement for 
this project 
PFMS (formerly CPSMS-Central Plan Scheme Monitoring 
System) is a web-based application of Government of India. 
It has established an online financial management 
information and decision support system for tracking of funds 
released and real time reporting of expenditure at all levels 
of program implementation through Treasury and Bank 
interface. PFMS provides various reports on geographical 
distribution of scheme-wise, sector-wise fund flows on a 
central platform. The system also has several links to 
financial networks of Central and State governments and 
gives real time reporting of expenditure at all levels of 
program implementation. PFMS has also developed an 
interface with the Core Banking Solution of Banks, whereby 
bank balances and transaction details of implementing 
agencies receiving grants from Government is available on a 
near real time basis for reconciliation and information from a 
single source. The purpose/objective is to provide greater 
transparency to the Govt. functioning at various levels and 
track funds up-to the last level thus minimizing intermediary 
delays and providing direct benefit to the beneficiaries. PFMS 
has also been entrusted with the enlarged scope of 
implementation of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) in many 
schemes of the Government. It is envisaged that integration 
of financial applications and process improvements planned 
under PFMS will result in better financial administration in 
Government. 
Scope of the work for which the Bidder 
was responsible 
• 
Prepare the technical solution architecture of an e-
Governance/mGovernance 
application/solution 
including the technology. 
• 
Proper documentation of the solution proposed 
SRS/FRD. 
• 
High level, low level and database design of the 
application proposed. 
• 
Deployment architecture. 
• 
Development and milestone plan. 
• 
Management of various phases of the application 
development 
including 
design, 
development, 
implementation, quality assurance and conformance 
to security requirements for application hosting at the 
data center.

developed for quality assurance and code review,

feature up-gradation and support of application 
software post completion of development related 
activities.

Project 5: Selection of Service Provider for providing professional services (IT, 
software development, Remote Sensing & GIS etc.) for development, 
improvement, operation, and maintenance of WIMS, India-WRIS, State-WRIS, 
GAL and ICCC 
Item (1) 
Particulars of the Project (2) 
Name of the Project 
Selection of Service Provider for providing professional 
services (IT, software development, Remote Sensing & GIS 
etc.) for development, improvement, operation, and 
maintenance of WIMS, India-WRIS, State-WRIS, GAL and 
ICCC 
Client Name for whom the services 
were provided 
National Water Informatics Centre 
Cost of the project 
7.2 Cr. 
Project Start Date / LOA 
August 2024 
Project End Date 
December 2025 
Nature of the Project (Give a brief 
description for better understanding 
relevant to experience requirement for 
this project 
Responsible 
for 
“Development 
of 
New 
Modules, 
Enhancement of Existing Modules and Operation and 
Maintenance of India-WRIS (Covering state-WRIS) and 
WIMS of NWIC”. The professionals provided by are broadly 
performing the below mentioned tasks: 
i. 
Development of New Modules: Analyzing the 
requirements of NWIC and developing new modules for 
India-WRIS and WIMS. This involves conducting a thorough 
assessment of the existing system, identifying gaps and 
areas for improvement, and designing and implementing new 
functionalities to meet the evolving needs of water resource 
management. 
ii. 
Enhancement of Existing Modules: Review the 
current modules of India-WRIS and WIMS and propose 
enhancements to optimize their performance, usability, and 
effectiveness. This involves refining data collection methods, 
improving data visualization and analysis tools, and 
incorporating advanced features to support better decision-
making in water resource planning and management. 
iii. 
Operation and Maintenance: Ongoing support for the 
operation and maintenance of India-WRIS and WIMS. This 
includes 
ensuring 
the 
system's 
smooth 
functioning, 
addressing technical issues, performing regular updates and 
upgrades, and maintaining data integrity and security. 
Monitoring the performance of the platforms, conducting 
periodic audits, and implementing necessary improvements. 
Scope of the work for which the Bidder 
was responsible 
1. Manpower deployment of 62 technical resources on 
open-source technology, PostgreSQL and GIS 
2. WIMS and India – WRIS Portal Maintenance and 
support, cloud, DB and application maintenance  
3. Open Data Portal Development: It involves creating 
a web-based platform to provide public access to 
data and resources, ensuring transparency and ease 
of use. It typically includes user-friendly interfaces, 
real-time updates, and integration with various data 
systems. 
4. GIS based Platform Development and Maintenance
pages.141:
Proposal for Providing IT Related Manpower to GEL 
141 
 
Project 6: Project Management Unit for Hello Meghalaya 
Item (1) 
Particulars of the Project (2) 
Name of the Project 
Project Management Unit for Hello Meghalaya 
Client Name for whom the services 
were provided 
Meghalayan Age Ltd 
Cost of the project 
2.41 Cr 
Project Start Date / LOA 
November 2022 
Project End Date 
November 2025 
Nature of the Project (Give a brief 
description for better understanding 
relevant to experience requirement for 
this project 
Meghalaya is a unique State with a very young population 
demography. In the age group of 15-29 there are more than 
11 lakhs of youth and accounts for 30.6 percent of the 
State's population. These youth need to be effectively 
engaged in nation building activities. 
Government believes that digital technologies must be 
leveraged to engage the citizens especially the youth. In 
order to achieve the same, the Government plans to 
implement a mobile app (Hello Meghalaya) that engages 
youth through entertainment related content and provides a 
channel for the Government to disseminate scheme 
awareness messages, behavioural change messages and 
make them aware of the rich cultural heritage of Meghalaya. 
The platform would also benefit preservation and promotion 
of local languages through video streaming of movies, 
music and other content in local languages - Garo, Khasi 
and Pnar.

pages.150:
Proposal for Providing IT Related Manpower to GEL 
150 
 
 
Assumptions:  
 
 
1. Resource Deployment for project management units   
It is assumed that the personnel and resources provided by GT for this project will work under 
the guidance, supervision, and direction of the client or their designated representatives on day-
to-day basis. GT acknowledges that any decisions, approvals, or directions provided by the 
client will be deemed as integral to the successful execution of the project.  
2. Adherence to client leave policies  
In the absence of a specific leave policy in the RFP, GT will adhere to the standard leave 
entitlements applicable to consultants working with Indian government clients, which include 
annual leave, public holidays, and sick leave as per government norms.   
3. Resource Provision and Liability for Delay in Deployment 
GT will provide resources in accordance with the qualifications and experience specified in the 
RFP. Any additional filtering beyond these requirements may result in delays to resource 
deployment, and GT will not be held liable for any liquidated damages (LD) or penalties arising 
from such delays. 
4. Client Responsibilities for Infrastructure and Travel 
The client shall ensure the provision of adequate infrastructure for resources, including seating 
arrangements, office space, and officer’s washroom facilities. Additionally, the client will be 
responsible for covering the costs associated with project-related travel. 
 
5. CVs of Manpower 
As clarified in corrigendum III dated 19.01.2026, “that bidders submit comprehensive and up-
to-date Curriculum Vitae (CVs) for all proposed IT personnel at the time of manpower 
deployment, as and when specific project requirements / deployment requests are Issued by 
GEL.”, CVs will be furnished at the time of manpower deployment.
